hypothesis: Across SciFact and NFCorpus, RRF at k=60 will improve nDCG@10 over the stronger individual lexical ranker while remaining invariant to a 100x relative score-scale sweep; an identical-ranker fusion control will show no gain.
claimBoundary: The evidence identifies score-scale robustness and diversity limits for exact unigram/bigram lexical retrieval on two small public BEIR test sets. It does not establish gains for dense retrievers, learned fusion, approximate indexes, latency, or a private production corpus.

controls: two datasets with fixed public test qrels
